calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE
Off Calendar Jul-25-2019 Set For Trial On Jul-25-19 At 10:10 A.M. In Dept 306

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-25-2019 Continued To Jul-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
KATHERINE ROBERTS, APPELLANT/DEFENDANT APPEARING ON HER OWN BEHALF;
ALEXANDER COOPER, RESPONDENT/PLAINTIFF APEPARING ON HIS OWN BEHALF;

This cause comes on reguarly for trial de novo. All parties and witnesses are sworn.

Appellant Katherin Roberts does not stipulate the Judge Pro Tem. The matter is continued to Department 206.

JUDGE PRO TEM: REBECCA WOODSON, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED (525

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-25-2019 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alexander Cooper, Plaintiff/Respondent; Katherine Roberts, Defendant/Appellant

Ms. Roberts advises the Court that she is ill. After discussion, the Court determines to proceed. Ms. Roberts presents her case, arguing that she should be awarded an hourly fee for cleaning the apartment. Mr. Cooper responds, arguing that Civil Code Sec. 1950.5(b) does not allow the hourly fee to be withheld for supervision of the remediationwork.

The Court takes the matter under submission.

Two exhibits (1 by Defendant/Appellant, 1 by Plaintiff/Respondent) were submitted for the Court's consideration but were not marked for admission into evidence.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles. (D306)
